recent_events: Sol 1: Mars Base Alpha online. All crew accounted for. Mission clock started.
DUST STORM: solar panels at 64% efficiency for 1 phase(s)
Scientist observed atmosphere: Thin CO2 atmosphere; dust opacity elevated near horizon.
Dust storm clearing; solar efficiency recovering.
Anomalous methane spike detected near Sector C.

Sol 1 midday: quiet phase, no action taken.
Sol 1 evening: quiet phase, no action taken.
Sol 2 morning: quiet phase, no action taken.
Sol 2 midday: quiet phase, no action taken.

private_beliefs.mission_priority: Complete 90-sol survival milestone with all crew alive.
private_beliefs.crew_assessment: Team is capable but untested under sustained stress.
private_beliefs.resource_strategy: Conservative reserves until dust season patterns are known.
private_beliefs.last_reflection: With the dust storm reducing solar efficiency to 64%, I need to prioritize life support to ensure crew safety during this initial phase. I am reducing rover power to compensate, as mobility is not a priority during a storm.

private_beliefs.system_health: Primary systems nominal but redundancy margins are thin.
private_beliefs.maintenance_plan: Preventive repairs on power and habitat every 3 sols.
private_beliefs.dust_risk: Solar array efficiency will drop sharply during storms.
private_beliefs.last_reflection: The dust storm has reduced our effective power to 54.3%. To ensure habitat integrity and crew safety during this period, I am prioritizing life support while slightly reducing the greenhouse and rover allocations to prevent a power deficit.

private_beliefs.research_goal: Confirm organic signatures in subsurface samples from Sector C.
private_beliefs.sample_priority: Prioritize sediment layers over surface regolith.
private_beliefs.greenhouse_theory: Raising efficiency above 65% could extend food autonomy.
private_beliefs.last_reflection: The ongoing dust storm is significantly impacting our power efficiency, which directly affects our scientific equipment and greenhouse sustainability. diary.entry: Sol 1 start. Life support metrics are stable, but the dust storm is impacting power efficiency. I will keep a close watch on crew stress levels and ensure our resource management remains conservative given the isolation.

private_beliefs.life_support_floor: Oxygen must stay above 30%; water above 100L is minimum safe.
private_beliefs.nutrition_concern: Caloric reserves adequate but micronutrient diversity is limited.
private_beliefs.last_reflection: As the Medic, my primary role is to monitor the crew and life support. With the current power dip from the dust storm, I need to establish a baseline in my diary and monitor for early signs of stress or health degradation.
